meta.title: Ralph Script Monitor
meta.description: A browser-based, offline-first application for executing, monitoring, and managing local scripts using an executable PRD.json model.

architecture.overview: Offline-first SPA with a local companion runner responsible for script execution and file monitoring.

constraints: User approval required before applying AI changes
All AI output must be schema validated

rules: All theming must be done via CSS

features.title: Codebase Setup & Tooling
features.description: Initialize and configure the application codebase with required frameworks, tooling, and conventions.

userStories.title: Initialize Frontend Project
acceptanceCriteria: React project initialized using Vite
TypeScript enabled
Project runs locally with a single start command

userStories.title: Configure Tailwind CSS v4
acceptanceCriteria: Tailwind CSS v4 installed
Tailwind imported via CSS using @import
No tailwind.config.js file exists
Dark mode works using class-based strategy

userStories.title: Install shadcn/ui
acceptanceCriteria: shadcn/ui installed and usable
At least one shadcn component renders correctly
Component styles respect Tailwind and dark mode rules

userStories.title: Configure Linting and Formatting
acceptanceCriteria: ESLint configured for React + TypeScript
Prettier configured and integrated
Lint and format commands run successfully
No lint errors on initial codebase

userStories.title: Setup Testing Framework
acceptanceCriteria: Testing framework installed (Jest or Vitest)
Sample test passes
Coverage reporting enabled
Tests can be run with a single command

userStories.title: Establish Project Structure
acceptanceCriteria: Standard folder structure created (components, features, hooks, utils)
Folder structure documented
Structure supports feature-based organization

features.title: Project Management
features.description: Create and manage projects that encapsulate scripts and their PRD.json definitions.

userStories.title: Create Project
acceptanceCriteria: User can create a project with name and description
Each project owns a single PRD.json
Project data is persisted locally

userStories.title: Delete Project
acceptanceCriteria: User can delete a project
Associated execution history is removed
Deletion requires confirmation

features.title: PRD.json Editor & Builder
features.description: Create and edit PRD.json using structured UI and AI assistance.

userStories.title: Visual PRD Editor
acceptanceCriteria: User can edit PRD.json sections via UI
Editor enforces schema validity
Invalid PRD.json cannot be saved

userStories.title: AI-Assisted PRD Editing
acceptanceCriteria: User can modify PRD.json via chat
AI suggestions require user approval
Changes are schema validated before apply

features.title: Script Execution & Monitoring
features.description: Execute local scripts and monitor logs and progress in real time.

userStories.title: Start and Stop Script Execution
acceptanceCriteria: User can start a script from the UI
User can stop a running script
Only one script runs per project at a time

userStories.title: Live Log Streaming
acceptanceCriteria: Stdout and stderr are streamed live
Logs update in near real time
Large logs are handled efficiently

userStories.title: Progress File Monitoring
acceptanceCriteria: progress.txt is monitored in real time
Progress updates are reflected in UI
Missing progress file does not crash execution

features.title: Feature & Story Execution
features.description: Execute user stories and features using AI agents based on PRD.json.

userStories.title: Execute User Story
acceptanceCriteria: User can execute a single user story
Agent receives scoped PRD context
Story status updates automatically

userStories.title: Execute Feature Group
acceptanceCriteria: User can execute all stories in a feature
Execution respects strategy and dependencies
Failures halt execution when configured

features.title: Alerts & Reporting
features.description: Notify users and export execution results.

userStories.title: Execution Alerts
acceptanceCriteria: Notifications are sent on configured events
Notification channels are configurable
Failures trigger alerts

userStories.title: Export Reports
acceptanceCriteria: Execution reports can be exported as JSON
Reports include PRD snapshot and results
Exports are stored locally

features.title: Kanban Board for Features & Stories
features.description: Visual kanban-style board for managing features and user stories with drag-and-drop support, inline editing, and priority-based ordering.

userStories.title: Kanban Board View with Four Columns
acceptanceCriteria: Board displays four columns: Backlog, Todo, Busy, Complete
Features are displayed as cards based on their status property
Features are populated from the PRD.json file
Each feature card shows story count and completion progress (x/y format)
Each feature card shows a progress bar visualizing completion percentage
UI is responsive and works on different screen sizes

userStories.title: Expandable Feature View
acceptanceCriteria: User can expand/collapse a feature card to show/hide its user stories
User stories are displayed as nested cards under the expanded feature
User stories show their status, priority, and title
Expanding one feature does not affect other features
Expand/collapse state is visually indicated (icon or chevron)

userStories.title: Feature Priority Ordering
acceptanceCriteria: Features are ordered by priority property (lower number = higher priority)
User can reorder features by dragging them up/down within their column
Reordering updates the priority property in PRD.json
Only features in 'todo' column are picked up by the agent for execution
Feature order is persisted to PRD.json

userStories.title: Drag and Drop Features Between Backlog and Todo
acceptanceCriteria: User can ONLY drag feature cards between Backlog and Todo columns
Cannot drag features from/to Busy or Complete columns
Dropping a feature between Backlog/Todo updates its status in PRD.json
Visual feedback during drag operation
Drag targets are only enabled for Backlog and Todo columns
Moving to Backlog sets status to 'backlog', moving to Todo sets status to 'todo'

userStories.title: Feature Detail View
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a feature card opens a detail modal/panel
Detail view shows full feature information (title, description, execution settings)
Detail view displays all user story cards for that feature
User can navigate between features without closing the modal
User can close detail view and return to board

userStories.title: Add New Story
acceptanceCriteria: User can add a new story to a feature
New stories default to status 'todo'
User can enter title, type, priority, and acceptance criteria
New story is saved to PRD.json under the correct feature

userStories.title: Edit Story Details
acceptanceCriteria: User can click a story card to edit its details
User can edit: title, type, priority, acceptanceCriteria
Editing is only allowed for stories with status 'todo'
Stories with status 'busy' or 'complete' are read-only
Changes are saved to PRD.json

userStories.title: Edit Feature Details
acceptanceCriteria: User can edit feature properties in the detail view
Editable fields: title, description, execution mode, execution strategy
Editing is only allowed for features with status 'todo' or 'backlog'
Features with status 'busy' or 'complete' are read-only
Changes are saved to PRD.json

features.title: Fix Kanban Board Status Mapping
features.description: Correct the status-to-column mapping and enforce editing restrictions based on status.

userStories.title: Fix Status-to-Column Mapping
acceptanceCriteria: Features/stories with status 'backlog' appear in Backlog column
Features/stories with status 'todo' appear in Todo column
Features/stories with status 'busy' appear in Busy column
Features/stories with status 'complete' appear in Complete column
No items appear in wrong columns
Column assignment is based on the status property from PRD.json

userStories.title: Enforce Editing Restrictions by Status
acceptanceCriteria: Only features/stories with status 'backlog' can be edited
Features/stories with status 'todo', 'busy', or 'complete' are read-only
Edit buttons/forms are disabled for non-backlog items
Visual indication shows which items are editable vs read-only
Attempting to edit non-backlog items shows appropriate message

userStories.title: New Items Default to Backlog
acceptanceCriteria: Newly created features default to status 'backlog'
Newly created stories default to status 'backlog'
New items appear in the Backlog column
New items are saved to PRD.json with status 'backlog'

userStories.title: Fix Screen size
acceptanceCriteria: Application must fill the entire screen.
When the board scrolls, only scroll the board area, not the entire page.
No white space should be visible around the application.
UI elements should be properly aligned and spaced.
The application layout should be responsive to different screen sizes.

features.title: Replace Kanban Modals with Left-Side Drawer
features.description: Replace Kanban board modal dialogs (Feature Detail, Add Story, Edit Story) with a unified left-side drawer component using shadcn/ui Sheet for improved UX and accessibility.

userStories.title: Install and Configure shadcn Sheet Component
acceptanceCriteria: shadcn/ui Sheet component installed and added to src/components/ui/sheet.tsx
Sheet component configured for left-side opening by default
Sheet component supports dark mode and respects Tailwind CSS v4 theming
Sheet component includes proper accessibility features (focus trap, ESC handling, ARIA)
Component exports include Sheet, SheetTrigger, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle, SheetDescription, SheetFooter

userStories.title: Create Shared Drawer Wrapper Component
acceptanceCriteria: AppDrawer wrapper component created at src/components/ui/app-drawer.tsx
Wrapper supports three size variants: sm (400px), md (600px), lg (800px)
Wrapper standardizes open/onOpenChange prop pattern
Wrapper includes consistent animation and transition behavior from left side
Wrapper provides header, content, and footer layout sections
Component is reusable across Kanban feature components

userStories.title: Replace FeatureDetailDialog with Drawer
acceptanceCriteria: FeatureDetailDialog migrated from Dialog to Sheet component (lg size)
Drawer opens from left side when feature card is clicked
All existing functionality preserved (title, description, execution settings display)
User story cards displayed correctly within drawer
Feature navigation (prev/next) works within drawer
Read-only state for busy/complete features preserved
Drawer closes on ESC key and backdrop click
No visual regressions in dark mode

userStories.title: Replace AddStoryDialog with Drawer
acceptanceCriteria: AddStoryDialog migrated from Dialog to Sheet component (md size)
Drawer opens from left side when 'Add Story' is clicked
All form fields work correctly (title, type, priority, acceptance criteria)
Form validation functions properly
New story is saved to PRD.json with correct feature association
New stories default to 'backlog' status
Drawer closes after successful save
Cancel button closes drawer without saving

userStories.title: Replace EditStoryDialog with Drawer
acceptanceCriteria: EditStoryDialog migrated from Dialog to Sheet component (md size)
Drawer opens from left side when story card is clicked
All editable fields work correctly (title, type, priority, acceptance criteria)
Read-only mode enforced for stories with status 'todo', 'busy', or 'complete'
Only stories with status 'backlog' can be edited
Changes are saved to PRD.json correctly
Visual indication shows when story is read-only
Drawer closes after successful save or on cancel

userStories.title: Handle Nested Drawer Behavior in Kanban
acceptanceCriteria: When AddStoryDialog opens from FeatureDetailDialog, FeatureDetail drawer closes
When EditStoryDialog opens from FeatureDetailDialog, FeatureDetail drawer closes
After saving/canceling AddStory or EditStory, FeatureDetail drawer reopens
State is preserved when returning to FeatureDetail drawer
Navigation flow feels natural and intuitive
No drawer stacking or z-index issues occur

userStories.title: Clean Up Old Kanban Dialog Code
acceptanceCriteria: Old Dialog imports removed from Kanban components
All Kanban components use Sheet/AppDrawer instead of Dialog
No unused Dialog-related code remains in Kanban feature
All Kanban functionality tested and working with drawer pattern
TypeScript builds without errors
ESLint passes with no warnings
No visual regressions on Kanban board

features.title: Wider Drawer and Add Feature Button
features.description: Increase drawer width to at least 1/3 of screen and add capability to create new features from the Kanban board via a button above the Backlog column.

userStories.title: Increase Drawer Width
acceptanceCriteria: Large (lg) drawer width updated to at least 1/3 of screen width (minimum 33.33vw or ~33%)
Drawer remains responsive and doesn't break on smaller screens
Content within drawer is properly visible and not cramped
Drawer width is consistent across all Kanban drawer instances (FeatureDetail, AddStory, EditStory)
Dark mode styling works correctly with wider drawer
Drawer animation is smooth at new width

userStories.title: Add 'Add Feature' Button Above Backlog Column
acceptanceCriteria: Button is positioned above the Backlog column header
Button has clear label (e.g., '+ Add Feature' or 'New Feature')
Button styling is consistent with application theme
Button is visible and accessible in both light and dark modes
Button has proper hover and focus states for accessibility
Button click opens the Add Feature drawer from left side

userStories.title: Create Add Feature Drawer Component
acceptanceCriteria: AddFeatureDrawer component created using Sheet component (lg size)
Drawer opens from left side when 'Add Feature' button is clicked
Form includes fields: title (required), description (required), execution mode (dropdown), execution strategy (dropdown)
Form includes field for initial status with default value 'backlog'
All form fields have proper labels and validation
Required field validation is enforced
Form has Save and Cancel buttons

userStories.title: Generate Feature ID and Save New Feature
acceptanceCriteria: New feature ID is auto-generated following existing pattern (F-XXX format)
Feature ID is unique and sequential (finds next available number)
New feature is saved to PRD.json in the features array
New feature includes empty userStories array
Default execution mode is 'group' if not specified
Default execution strategy is 'sequential' if not specified
New feature appears immediately in Backlog column after save
Drawer closes after successful save

userStories.title: Handle Add Feature Form Validation and Errors
acceptanceCriteria: Empty title shows validation error message
Empty description shows validation error message
Save button is disabled when form is invalid
Error messages are clearly visible and accessible
Cancel button works and closes drawer without saving
Form resets when drawer is closed and reopened
Success message or feedback shown after feature is created
Focus is properly managed when drawer opens and closes

features.title: Backend PRD.json Persistence Service
features.description: Create a backend service running via concurrently that provides API endpoints for loading and saving PRD.json files to the user's machine based on project meta.folder names.

userStories.title: Setup Backend Service with Concurrently
acceptanceCriteria: Backend service created using Node.js/Express or similar
Concurrently package installed and configured in package.json
npm/yarn scripts updated to run frontend and backend simultaneously
Backend runs on a separate port (e.g., 3001) from frontend
CORS configured to allow frontend requests
Backend starts automatically when running dev command
Backend structure follows best practices (routes, controllers, services)

userStories.title: Create File System Service for PRD.json
acceptanceCriteria: File service module created to handle read/write operations
Service determines file path using pattern: {meta.folder}.json
Service validates PRD.json against schema before saving
Service handles file system errors gracefully (file not found, permissions, etc.)
Service creates backup of existing file before overwriting
Service supports configurable base directory for PRD files
File operations are async and non-blocking

userStories.title: Implement Load PRD.json API Endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET endpoint created at /api/prd/:folderName
Endpoint reads PRD.json file from {folderName}.json
Returns parsed JSON content with proper content-type header
Returns 404 status if file doesn't exist
Returns 500 status with error message on file read errors
Validates JSON structure before returning
Endpoint is tested and working with sample PRD files

userStories.title: Implement Save PRD.json API Endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: POST/PUT endpoint created at /api/prd/:folderName
Endpoint accepts PRD.json data in request body
Validates that meta.folder matches :folderName parameter
Validates PRD.json against schema before saving
Writes file to {folderName}.json on user's machine
Returns success response with saved data confirmation
Returns 400 status for validation errors with detailed messages
Returns 500 status for file write errors
File is formatted (pretty-printed) when saved

userStories.title: Create Project List API Endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: GET endpoint created at /api/projects
Endpoint scans configured directory for .json files
Returns list of available projects with meta information
Each project includes: folder name, title, description, status from meta
Filters out invalid or corrupted JSON files
Returns empty array if no projects found
Response includes count of available projects

userStories.title: Integrate Frontend with Backend API
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend API client/service created for backend communication
Project loading uses backend API instead of localStorage/IndexedDB
PRD.json edits trigger save API call to backend
Loading states shown while fetching from backend
Error handling displays user-friendly messages for API failures
Frontend falls back gracefully if backend is unavailable
All existing features work with new backend integration
No data loss during transition from local storage to file system

userStories.title: Add Auto-save and Conflict Resolution
acceptanceCriteria: Changes to PRD.json auto-save after short debounce period (e.g., 2 seconds)
Visual indicator shows save status (saving, saved, error)
File watcher detects external changes to PRD.json files
User is notified if file was modified externally
Option to reload from disk or keep current version on conflict
Manual save button available for immediate save
Unsaved changes warning shown before closing/navigating away

features.title: Toggle Kanban Board Between Features and User Stories
features.description: Add the ability to switch the kanban board view between displaying features and displaying individual user stories, allowing users to manage at different levels of granularity.

userStories.title: Add View Toggle Control
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle control added to kanban board header/toolbar
Toggle has two options: 'Features' and 'User Stories'
Toggle styling is consistent with application theme
Toggle is accessible via keyboard navigation
Active view is clearly indicated visually
Toggle works in both light and dark modes

userStories.title: Display User Stories in Kanban Columns
acceptanceCriteria: When 'User Stories' view is selected, individual user stories appear as cards
User stories are distributed across columns based on their status property
Each user story card displays: title, type, priority, parent feature name
User story cards are visually distinct from feature cards
User stories from all features are shown in the board
Empty columns show appropriate empty state
Column headers remain: Backlog, Todo, Busy, Complete

userStories.title: User Story Card Interactions
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a user story card opens edit/detail drawer
User story drawer shows all story details and parent feature info
Read-only enforcement applies (only backlog stories editable)
User can navigate to parent feature from story detail view
Drawer behavior consistent with feature detail drawer
User story status badge displays correctly on card

userStories.title: Drag and Drop User Stories
acceptanceCriteria: User stories can be dragged between Backlog and Todo columns only
Cannot drag stories from/to Busy or Complete columns
Dragging updates the user story status in PRD.json
Visual feedback during drag operation
Drag behavior is consistent with feature drag behavior
Parent feature is not affected when dragging individual stories

userStories.title: Add Filtering and Grouping for User Story View
acceptanceCriteria: Filter control allows filtering by feature (show stories from specific feature)
Filter control allows filtering by story type (setup, feature, bugfix, etc.)
Option to group user stories by parent feature within each column
Clear/reset filter button to show all stories
Active filters are visually indicated
Filter state persists when switching between views

userStories.title: Add User Story from Board
acceptanceCriteria: Add Story button appears above Backlog column in User Stories view
Button click opens Add Story drawer
Form includes dropdown to select parent feature (required)
Form includes all standard user story fields
New story defaults to 'backlog' status
New story appears immediately in Backlog column after save
Cannot add story if no features exist (show message)

userStories.title: Persist View Preference and Maintain State
acceptanceCriteria: Selected view (Features/User Stories) persists in localStorage
Board remembers last selected view on page reload
Switching views preserves scroll position when returning
Column collapse/expand states are view-specific
Active filters reset when switching views (or persist separately)
View toggle transition is smooth and performant
No data loss occurs when switching between views
